---
role: proof
locale: en
of_concept: extension-of-commuting-torus-elements
source_book: gtm020
source_chapter: "9"
source_section: "8"
---

**Proof.** Let $A$ be the closed subgroup of $G$ generated by $T$ and $s$, and let $T_0$ be the connected component of the identity in $A$. Since $T$ and $s$ commute with each other, $A$ is abelian. The subgroup $T_0$ is connected, compact, and abelian, and being a closed subgroup of a Lie group, it is itself a Lie group. By the characterization of tori (compact, abelian, connected Lie group), $T_0$ is a torus subgroup of $G$.

The quotient $A/T_0$ is a compact discrete group, hence finite. Let $A'$ be the subgroup of $A$ generated by $T_0$ and $s$. Since $A/T_0$ is finite, $A'$ is a finite extension of $T_0$, and the image of $s$ in the finite quotient has finite order. By the monic property of tori, $A'$ has a generator $x$, i.e., the cyclic subgroup generated by $x$ is dense in $A'$. Therefore, the closure of this cyclic subgroup is a compact, connected, abelian Lie group containing both $T_0$ and $s$, which must be a torus $T'$. We have $T \subset T_0 \subset T'$ and $s \in T'$. $\square$
